title: Fails to initialize machine "SSH key already exists"
body:
### Issue Description

`podman machine init --now my-machine` returns
```
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug
DEBU[0000] Using Podman machine with `qemu` virtualization provider
Extracting compressed file: my-machine_fedora-coreos-39.20240128.2.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2: done
Error: SSH key already exists: /Users/johns/.ssh/my-machine
DEBU[0021] Shutting down engines
```
However, the machine does not exist `podman machine ls` shows nothing and...
```
$ podman machine start my-machine
Error:  my-machine: VM does not exist
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman 4.9.0 on mac (m1 pro, 14.2.1)
2. Initialize & start a machine with a name
3. *I think the issue must arise on an unclean QEMU stop/start*, so either sleep, restart, or shutdown the laptop
4. Try to initialize a machine with the same name again after a restart
5. Uninstalling Podman and deleting the directories with SSH keys, then installing Podman again solves this issue.


### Describe the results you received

`Error: SSH key already exists: /Users/johns/.ssh/my-machine` and the `machine init` command errors out

### Describe the results you expected

The machine gets initialized and starts. (I don't think it matters what a previous SSH key was, either re-use it or create a new one?)

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

m1 pro, macOS Sonoma 14.2.1 laptop which I frequently restart & shutdown

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This should not longer be a problem with 5.X
Thanks!! To confirm, I have not received this error for a while with newer versions.
